typography.$description: Consolidated 8-style functional typography system. Reduced from 15 styles to eliminate redundancy and simplify design decisions. All body/UI text now uses lineHeight.normal (1.5) for improved breathability.

display.$description: Hero headlines and landing-page displays. Reserved for top-level page titles and marketing moments. Use sparingly.

heading1.$description: Primary section headings (h1). Use for main page sections and major content divisions.

heading2.$description: Secondary section headings (h2). Use for subsections and nested content layers.

heading3.$description: Component and card headers (formerly 'title'). Use for standalone titles within components, card headers, and UI section labels. Semibold to reduce visual weight.

body.$description: Body text for long-form prose, articles, and narrative content. 16px with 1.5 line-height for optimal readability. Use as the default text style.

ui.$description: Interface and secondary text. Use for sidebars, navigation, labels, captions, tooltips, and all non-body UI text. Replaces caption, label, body2 roles. 1.5 line-height for consistency and breathability.

technical.$description: Technical and code-adjacent text. Monospace font for token keys, state names, property names, code blocks, and metric readouts. Merges labelTechnical and monoSm. Compact line-height (1.0) appropriate for code.

badge.$description: Badge and tag labels only. Minimal size for metadata callouts and status indicators. Use only when space is severely constrained; otherwise prefer 'ui'.
